segments.text: Then Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to His disciples,
The scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whatever they tell you to observe, that observe and do, but do not do according to their works; for they say, and do not do.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For they bind heavy burdens, hard to bear, and lay them on men's shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But all their works they do to be seen by men. They make their phylacteries broad and enlarge the borders of their garments.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They love the best places at feasts, the best seats in the synagogues,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: greetings in the marketplaces, and to be called by men, 'Rabbi, Rabbi.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But you, do not be called 'Rabbi'; for One is your Teacher, the Christ, and you are all brethren.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Do not call anyone on earth your father; for One is your Father, He who is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And do not be called teachers; for One is your Teacher, the Christ.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But he who is greatest among you shall be your servant.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you shut up the kingdom of heaven against men; for you neither go in yourselves, nor do you allow those who are entering to go in.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you devour widows' houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. Therefore you will receive greater condemnation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel land and sea to win one proselyte, and when he is won, you make him twice as much a son of hell as yourselves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, blind guides, who say, 'Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the temple, he is obliged to perform it.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Fools and blind! For which is greater, the gold or the temple that sanctifies the gold?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: 'Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift that is on it, he is obliged to perform it.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Fools and blind! For which is greater, the gift or the altar that sanctifies the gift?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore he who swears by the altar, swears by it and by all things on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He who swears by the temple, swears by it and by Him who dwells in it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he who swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God and by Him who sits on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin, and have neglected the weightier matters of the law: justice and mercy and faith. These you ought to have done, without leaving the others undone.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Blind guides, who strain out a gnat and swallow a camel!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you cleanse the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of extortion and self-indulgence.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Blind Pharisee, first cleanse the inside of the cup and dish, that the outside of them may be clean also.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs which indeed appear beautiful outwardly, but inside are full of dead men's bones and all uncleanness.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even so you also outwardly appear righteous to men, but inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! Because you build the tombs of the prophets and adorn the monuments of the righteous,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and say, 'If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore you are witnesses against yourselves that you are sons of those who murdered the prophets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers' guilt.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Serpents, brood of vipers! How can you escape the condemnation of hell?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore, indeed, I send you prophets, wise men, and scribes: some of them you will kill and crucify, and some of them you will scourge in your synagogues and persecute from city to city,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: that on you may come all the righteous blood shed on the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah, son of Berechiah, whom you murdered between the temple and the altar.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Assuredly, I say to you, all these things will come upon this generation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the one who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were not willing!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: See! Your house is left to you desolate;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: for I say to you, you shall see Me no more till you say, 'Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!'
segments.speaker: Jesus
